story.heading:
Kept in touch
during the pandemic
story.body: When the world slowed down, we didn't. Late-night calls and care packages kept us close across the distance.

story.heading:
Reconnected
in the Poconos
story.body: A weekend in the mountains turned into something neither of us expected — the start of everything.

story.heading:
Got engaged in
Hudson, NY
story.body: Overlooking the river, on a perfect autumn afternoon, Matt asked the question. Sarah said yes.

story.heading:
Sarah moved to
Massachusetts
story.body: New city, new chapter. Boxes and furniture and a lot of excited energy for what comes next.

story.heading: More to come
story.body: The story keeps writing itself. Stay tuned for what happens next.

story.heading:
August 28th,
Lancaster PA
story.body: Friends, family, and everyone we love gathered in one place to celebrate with us. We can't wait.

celebration.heading: Our Celebration
celebration.description: Join us as we celebrate in the heart of Lancaster County. We can't wait to share this day with you!
celebration.eventDetails: A cocktail hour and reception will follow the ceremony. More details to come — check back closer to the date.

infoRows.label: Weather & Attire
infoRows.body: August in Lancaster is warm and humid — expect highs around 85°F. We suggest semi-formal attire and comfortable shoes for the outdoor ceremony.

infoRows.label: Transportation & Parking
infoRows.body: Free street parking is available near the venue. We'll share shuttle information closer to the date for guests staying downtown.

infoRows.label: Logistics & Accessibility
infoRows.body: Excelsior is fully accessible. If you have any questions or need accommodations, please reach out and we'll be happy to help.

travel.heading: Travel & Stay
travel.body: Lancaster is a beautiful destination with plenty to explore. Here are our recommendations to make your visit memorable.

whereToStay.heading: Lancaster Arts Hotel
whereToStay.body: Our top pick for out-of-town guests — art-forward, right downtown, walking distance to everything.

whereToStay.heading:
Lancaster Marriott
at Penn Square
whereToStay.body: Right on Penn Square, easy for everyone to find. Modern amenities in the heart of the city.

whereToStay.heading: Cork Factory Hotel
whereToStay.body: A converted 19th-century cork factory with a lot of character, tucked along the river.

whereToStay.heading:
The Inn at
Leola Village
whereToStay.body: A restored 1920s village in the countryside, about 10 minutes from the city.

whereToStay.heading:
Eden Resort
& Suites
whereToStay.body: Pools, spa, and a full-service restaurant — great for families or anyone making a weekend of it.

whereToStay.heading:
Holiday Inn
Lancaster
whereToStay.body: Solid, affordable, convenient — a good pick for guests who just need a clean place to sleep.

whereToEat.heading: 401 Prime
whereToEat.body: Downtown's benchmark for a proper night out — dry-aged cuts, serious wine list, no shortcuts.

whereToEat.heading: Luca
whereToEat.body: Our go-to for handmade pasta and a room that actually feels special.

whereToEat.heading: Plough
whereToEat.body: Seasonal and locally sourced inside the Lancaster Arts Hotel — quietly one of the best meals in the city.

whereToEat.heading: Southern Market
whereToEat.body: A beautifully restored 1888 market with globally-inspired chef stations and a great central bar.

whereToEat.heading:
The Lancaster
Dispensing Co.
whereToEat.body: "DipCo" to locals — Victorian bar, cold beer, live music on weekends, here since 1978.

whereToEat.heading:
Spring House
Brewing Co.
whereToEat.body: Local craft staple with a full food menu and a tap list worth exploring.

whereToEat.heading: Square One Coffee
whereToEat.body: Award-winning family-owned micro-roaster on Duke Street — single origins, seasonal espresso, hidden garden courtyard out back.

whereToEat.heading:
Rachel's Café
& Creperie
whereToEat.body: Freshly made sweet and savory crêpes, great coffee, covered patio — perfect before a busy day.

whereToEat.heading: Route 30 Diner
whereToEat.body: 24 hours, giant pancakes, all-day breakfast — the reliable call when someone needs eggs at 11pm.

activities.heading:
Lancaster
Central Market
activities.body: Open Tue, Fri & Sat in the heart of downtown — local produce, PA Dutch specialties, and the best way to spend a slow morning.

activities.heading: The Amish Village
activities.body: Farmhouse tours, a 12-acre village, and backroads bus tours through working Amish countryside — ask for AJ, Sarah's brother and one of their best guides.

activities.heading: Strasburg Rail Road
activities.body: A 45-minute ride through rolling Amish farmland on a working steam locomotive. Genuinely great, not just a tourist thing.

activities.heading: Downtown Lancaster
activities.body: A guided walk through 300 years of history — churches, colonial architecture, and streets most visitors walk right past.

activities.heading:
Landis Valley
Village & Farm
activities.body: 100 acres of Pennsylvania German rural life with working trades and open fields — best in summer when everything is active.

activities.heading: kayakLanCo
activities.body: Rent a kayak and paddle the Susquehanna. Late August is peak season — calm water, great scenery, easy half-day trip.

activities.heading: Waltz Vineyards
activities.body: A laid-back Lancaster County winery with great outdoor seating — built for a slow afternoon in the sun.

activities.heading: Long's Park
activities.body: Outdoor concerts on the amphitheater lawn all summer long — bring a blanket, grab food nearby, stay for the evening.

activities.heading: Covered bridge drive
activities.body: Lancaster County has 28 intact covered bridges. Pick up a map and spend an afternoon getting pleasantly lost.

rsvp.heading: Let us know if you can make it
rsvp.body: We'd love to know if you can make it. Please RSVP by June 1, 2026 so we can plan accordingly.

registry.heading: Registry
registry.body: Your presence is the greatest gift, but if you wish to celebrate with us, we've registered at the following:
